page: Google Cloud Platform c4d-standard-192-lssd is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the c4d family. It features 192 dedicated virtual CPUs and 744.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of approximately 3.88 GB of RAM per core. While the basic description notes the inclusion of 16 local SSDs, the explicit local storage size is designated as none in the configuration parameters. There are no graphics processing units integrated into this instance type, and it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Operating with a dedicated CPU allocation, this instance provides predictable compute resources without the performance variability associated with shared-core environments. The high vCPU count and balanced memory-to-core ratio position this server class for large-scale parallel processing tasks. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high density of dedicated compute resources and memory allows for consolidation of massive workloads onto a single node, though the lack of GPU acceleration limits its utility for hardware-accelerated machine learning tasks.

The resource profile of the c4d-standard-192-lssd is suited for compute-heavy and memory-balanced workloads. This includes large-scale web serving, continuous integration and continuous delivery (CI/CD) pipelines, high-throughput enterprise applications, and distributed data processing tasks that do not require specialized GPU hardware.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4d-standard-192-lssd is an x86_64 architecture server within the c4d family, configured with 192 dedicated vCPUs and 744.0 GB of RAM. This configuration provides a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 3.88 GB per vCPU. While the basic configuration description references 16 local SSDs, the local storage size is formally listed as none, and the instance contains no GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. With its dedicated CPU allocation, the server delivers stable, predictable performance for highly parallelized tasks. It represents a high-density compute resource suitable for enterprise workloads, large-scale web serving, and distributed data processing. The lack of GPU acceleration and public IPv4 addresses are key architectural tradeoffs to consider when deploying this instance.
